snippet.title: The Inside Story Of Disney And Bob Iger's Succession Chaos
snippet.description:
CNBC’s Alex Sherman reports on the inside story of a CEO succession plan gone awry at Disney— a cautionary tale about ego and hubris at the highest levels of corporate America. Sherman spoke with  more than two dozen people who worked closely with Bob Iger and Bob Chapek between 2020 and 2022.

Reporting by: Alex Sherman
Supervising Producer: Jeff Morganteen
Camera: Timothy Fadek
Graphics by: Alex Wood

snippet.title: The Fall of Chapek: Three Sins that Cost Him the Disney Throne
snippet.description:
Following SEC Reports from The Walt Disney Company as they battle Nelson Peltz and Trian, we now have a fairly strong understanding of what cost former CEO Bob Chapek his job. Robert Iger was gunning for him from the get-go, but it was three errors by Chapek that allowed people like Christine McCarthy and organizations like Walkout Disney to take him down.

Join WDW Pro and @ValliantRenegade as they go through the big three "sins". As you watch, remember to keep these in your memories lest you also fall prey to movements in your company such as Reimagine Tomorrow, that may be virtuous in the eyes of many, but may also lead to your demise if you lose control.

Sin #1: 4:40
Sin #2: 14:47

snippet.title: Disney vs. the Battle of the Bobs | Crash Course
snippet.description:
Imagine you’re Bob Iger. You relinquish Disney’s CEO suite in 2020 with a sterling legacy intact. Smart, game-changing acquisitions. A good culture. Steady, imaginative leadership. Soaring profits and a jumbo stock price. And a sort of bet-the-ranch move into streaming that puts profit-making aside in favor of reinventing the entertainment platform your business rests upon. All is going swimmingly when you turn things over to your successor, Bob Chapek. You title your autobiography “The Ride of a Lifetime.” What a way to go. Then, boom. Covid lockdowns hit just weeks after you step down. Disney’s business sputters. Chapek alienates your team and the board. And that streaming bet unravels. What do you do? You roll back in as CEO less than three years after you left the company. Chapek is pushed out. How often does something like that happen? Like, never. Even Steve Jobs waited 11 years to make his roundtrip as Apple’s CEO.


There are lots of collisions to sort out here. That’s why I rang up my pal at Bloomberg Opinion, Beth Kowitt. Beth’s a veteran business writer and she spends a lot of time thinking about the mysteries of corporate America. 

See omnystudio.com/listener (https://omnystudio.com/listener) for privacy information.


Hosted by Bloomberg Opinion senior executive editor Tim O'Brien, Crash Course will bring listeners directly into the arenas where epic business and social upheavals occur. Every week, Crash Course will explore the lessons to be learned when creativity and ambition collide with competition and power -- on Wall Street and Main Street, and in Hollywood and Washington.

snippet.title: Can Bob Iger revive Disney? | FT Film
snippet.description:
For 15 years Bob Iger was the 'King of Hollywood', transforming Disney with the game-changing acquisitions of Marvel, Pixar and Lucasfilm. His successor Bob Chapek's disastrous tenure lasted just two years and now Iger is back for a second reign as CEO, confronted by a new landscape: Disney's legacy businesses are in decline, its streaming services are losing money and its share price has slumped. Can the king revive the Magic Kingdom?

snippet.title: Disney CEO Bob Iger calls Met Gala 'the happiest night on earth'
snippet.description:
(2 May 2023) 
MORE OF THIS INTERVIEW: 4432253
ASSOCIATED PRESS
New York, 1 May 2023
1. SOUNDBITE (English) Bob Iger, Disney CEO:
(Reporter: "You're at a place that, you know, some consider the happiest place on earth, one of your places. What is it like at the Met Gala?"
"Well, it's it's not, it's close to the happiest place. It's the happiest night on earth."
(Reporter: "Lastly, what can you say about the lawsuit in Florida?")
Iger: Nothing more than we've already said. Sorry. Thank you."

snippet.title: Bob Iger HUMILIATED, as his Mismanagement and stacking of Disney's Board is Exposed!
snippet.description:
#BobIger #BobChapek #Disney

There was a time, not too long ago, when Hollywood was enamored with Bob Iger, the man who brought Marvel and Star Wars to Disney – but that period is now over. His comments against the strike were not well received, and now, a major exposé from CNBC has revealed how badly he botched the transition of power to Bob Chapek.
 There is a enough to cover here that we have to do it over the course of several videos, but in this first one, we will break down how Iger never intended for Chapek to really run the company, why the board didn’t stop Iger when the alarm bells should have been ringing, and finally, the rumor we heard why Iger deemed it necessary to step back in the first place!

Check out our interview with Graham Hancock:
https://youtu.be/cjNkMn_VLho

snippet.title: The Corporate Battle for Disney’s Soul: Bob Iger, Blackrock and Nelson Peltz Explained
snippet.description:

As it turned out, the rumored cold war behind the scenes of Lucasfilm, Star Wars and Marvel, was but one front in the real conflict taking place in Disney’s highest echelons, and that conflict heated up significantly with the sudden ouster of Bob Chapek and all his lieutenants, and more recently, with the drama over certain boardmembers, where even blackrock got involved! 

Clearly, a game of corporate game of thrones is being played out before our eyes, and the stakes are nothing short of Disney’s soul, if you will, and the future direction of all of Disney’s assets.

But for us on the outside, it is to hard grasp what is really going on, and even who the key players are, let alone their intentions for the company. 
In this video then, we’re going to cut through all the noise, and in the briefest way possible bring you up to speed with what has happened behind the scenes at Disney’s corporate level, which new players have entered the game, and what this all means for Disney, the parks, Star Wars, Pixar and even Marvel going forward!

snippet.title: Disney BOMBSHELL | Why Chapek Was Fired? | The Iger v Peltz Saga
snippet.description:
BREAKING NEWS : Disney WARS BOMBSHELL is Pure INSANITY | Is THIS Why Former Disney CEO Bob Chapek Was Fired? | Nelson Peltz VS Bob Iger & The Walt Disney Company Board of Directors

Business & Financial News : Nelson Peltz Train Group vs Bob Iger and Disney Board | Proxy Fight | Bob Chapek Fired | Issac ( Ike ) Perlmutter Involved | Marvel Revenge | Disney CFO Christine McCarthy NEXT to be Fired by Bob Iger? | Walt Disney Company SEC Filing for Annual Stockholder Meeting 2023

With the latest Walt Disney Company Annual Stockholder Preliminary Report filed on Jan 17th, 2023 with the United States Securities & Exchange Commission (SEC), there is more coming to light regarding not only the fight between billionaire investor Nelson Peltz of the Trian Management Group, but also his relationship to the final days of terminated, former Disney CEO Bob Chapek. How were the then-restored Disney CEO Bob Iger and Disney Chief Financial Officer Christine McCarthy involved along with other players like Ike Perlmutter, Chairman of Marvel Entertainment?

This story is continuously compounding at every possible turn, twisting into an apparent web of tangled deception and corporate backstabbing. 

Nelson Peltz has a history of being a massive fundraiser for the very types of politicians that many would believe a man like Robert Iger would not favor. Peltz also seemed very adamant that Bob Chapek’s tenure at Disney needed to be secured to turn the company around over time, and it was Ike Perlmutter (Marvel) who feared that if Mr. Peltz of Train was not put on Disney’s Board of Directors, Bob Iger would regain power in the House of Mouse.

and @WDW_Pro of ThatParkPlace.com
